name: Hayedo de las Ilces Trail
name_original: Hayedo de Las Ilces — circular por el Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
description: Hike the moderate 6.8-mile Hayedo de las Ilces Trail in Picos de Europa, exploring ancient beech forests and charming villages.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a landscape characterized by the Picos de Europa National Park, featuring limestone peaks, deep gorges, and lush forests. The region is shaped by the Deva River, which flows through varied terrain, offering diverse natural features for exploration. This area provides a range of easy routes suitable for those seeking accessible outdoor experiences amidst mountain scenery and charming rural areas. Best easy hiking trails in Lic-Rio Deva The most popular easy hiking route is Lebaniego Way, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This path offers a gentle ascent through the Liébana region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de Santa Catalina – San Miguel Viewpoint loop from Potes, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This route provides views of Potes and the surrounding valleys, with a total elevation gain of 712 feet (217 meters). Local hikers also love the Infantado Tower – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through the picturesque village of Potes, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Hiking in Lic-Rio Deva is defined by river valleys, forested paths, and views of the Picos de Europa, with options for different ability levels. The network of easy hiking trails Lic-Rio Deva offers accessible routes for all. The routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 4,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Deva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Lic-Rio Deva offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 75 routes specifically categorized as easy. These trails allow you to explore the region's diverse landscapes, including the Picos de Europa National Park, without requiring extensive hiking experience.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Easy hikes in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a varied landscape shaped by the Deva River and the dramatic Picos de Europa National Park. You'll find paths through lush forests, along river valleys, and routes offering views of limestone peaks and charming rural areas. The region is known for its stunning mountain scenery and well-preserved natural environment.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The easy hiking trails in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible routes, the beautiful views of the Picos de Europa, and the opportunity to explore charming villages like Potes.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Catalina – San Miguel Viewpoint loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt; offers views of Potes and the surrounding valleys. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the picturesque village itself.

Q: Are the easy trails in Lic-Rio Deva suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easy trails in Lic-Rio Deva are well-suited for families. They typically feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, allowing children to enjoy the natural beauty of the Picos de Europa and the Deva River region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206575654/lebaniego-way" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebaniego Way&lt;/a&gt; offer a gentle experience through the Liébana region.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: While many areas are dog-friendly, it's important to be aware of specific regulations, especially within the Picos de Europa National Park. Always keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Check local signage or park authority guidelines for any restricted areas before you go.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see on easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes in Lic-Rio Deva offer access to various natural features and viewpoints. You can enjoy views of the dramatic limestone peaks of the Picos de Europa, explore lush forests, and follow the course of the Deva River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Catalina – San Miguel Viewpoint loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides excellent panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible on easy hikes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/991425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4135071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower&lt;/a&gt; are easily accessible, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt; taking you right through them. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Lebeña Church&lt;/a&gt;, a notable religious site in the area.

Q: How long do easy hikes in Lic-Rio Deva typically take?
A: The duration of easy hikes varies, but many can be completed in a few hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206575654/lebaniego-way" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebaniego Way&lt;/a&gt; is about 4 miles (6.5 km) and takes around 2 hours, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt; is 2.2 miles (3.6 km) and can be done in just over an hour.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hiking trails in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns and villages that serve as trailheads, such as Potes, Espinama, and Fuente Dé. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot, especially for popular routes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach easy hiking trails?
A: Public transport options exist, connecting key towns like Potes. However, access to specific trailheads, especially those deeper within the Picos de Europa National Park, might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving for more flexibility.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, towns like Potes, Espinama, and Fuente Dé offer a range of amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options, from hotels to guesthouses, making them excellent bases for exploring the easy hiking trails in the Lic-Rio Deva region.

Q: When is the best time of year to go easy hiking in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The spring and autumn months (April-June and September-October) are generally considered the best times for easy hiking in Lic-Rio Deva. The weather is typically mild, and the landscapes are vibrant with spring blooms or autumn colors. Summer can also be pleasant, though some trails might be busier.
tips: This beautiful town is located in the beautiful region of Liébana, at the foot of the majestic Picos de Europa. There are an infinite number of trail running and hiking routes.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Hayedo de las Ilces Trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route typically takes between 3 hours 30 minutes and 4 hours 30 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Hayedo de las Ilces Trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. While it's a comfortable walk, it does include a short, steep climb at certain points. It's best suited for those with some hiking experience. Hiking shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: Where does the Hayedo de las Ilces Trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The circular trail typically starts and ends in the charming village of Espinama. Parking is usually available in or near the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is predominantly unpaved, with approximately 95% natural terrain. You'll hike through dense and magnificent beech and oak forests, along the Pierga path in the Deva valley, across grasslands, and over various smaller valleys and streams. Expect a mix of gentle descents and some short, steep climbs.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty, especially the well-preserved beech and oak forests that give the trail its name. The scenery is known for its vibrant colors throughout the seasons. The route also offers views of the Coriscao peak and passes through traditional villages like Espinama and Las Ilces, where you'll find the Church of Saint James of Las Ilces.

faqs.question: Is the Hayedo de las Ilces Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are permitted on the trail. Please ensure they are kept under control and follow any local regulations regarding leashes, especially given the wildlife in the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers vibrant colors throughout the different seasons, making it beautiful year-round. Autumn is particularly popular for the stunning foliage of the beech and oak forests.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Picos de Europa National Park. However, always check the official park regulations before your visit, especially concerning activities like camping or specific access restrictions that might apply to certain areas or seasons.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Hayedo de Las Ilces trail, the Ruta de la Reconquista (Etapa 4), the Vadinian Way (Camino Vadiniense), and the Transcantábrica.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The area is a refuge for diverse wildlife, including some elusive species. It's recognized as one of the best-preserved environments in the Picos de Europa and is known to be home to some of the rare Cantabrian brown bears, though sightings are uncommon. You'll also encounter rich biodiversity in the flora, particularly the majestic, often ancient beech trees.

faqs.question: How is the trail marked?
faqs.answer: The Hayedo de las Ilces Trail is well-marked with white and yellow blazes, corresponding to the PR - PNPE 26 designation.

waypoints.name: Church of Saint James of Las Ilces
